# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  require 'image_size'

  def convert_image
    file = params[:file]
    image_format = params[:format]
    width = params[:width] || nil
    height = params[:height] || nil
    resize_method = params[:resize_method]

    # Create temporary directory and write file to it
    temp_dir = "#{Rails.root}/tmp/images"
    Dir.mkdir(temp_dir) unless Dir.exist?(temp_dir)
    filename = "image#{Time.now.to_i}.jpg"
    filepath = File.join(temp_dir, filename)
    IO.copy_stream(file, filepath)

    # Convert image with ImageMagick
    command = [
      "convert",
      filepath,
      "-format #{image_format}",
      "-resize #{width}x#{height}" if width && height,
      "#{Rails.root}/public/images/#{filename}"
    ]

    system(*command)
  end
end
